page: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz, the system allocates 16 physical cores with a single thread per core on a shared CPU basis. The hardware includes 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a single complimentary public IPv4 address, with no integrated GPU.

Benchmark data indicates highly polarized CPU performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU test registers in the top tier, placing in the top 10%, whereas multi-core stress-ng performance falls into the average category. Memory performance is robust, with the PassMark memory score ranking in the top 25%, supported by an uncached large-block read bandwidth of 131.64 GB/sec. Small-model LLM text generation also performs in the top 25%, while larger model inference and database operations remain in the average tier.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a clear tradeoff, offering a cost-efficient entry point for memory-intensive workloads but introducing potential resource contention under sustained multi-core loads. This resource density, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU alongside a substantial 1600 GB NVMe SSD, makes it highly efficient for memory-bound tasks that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU execution.

This configuration is appropriate for large-scale caching layers, in-memory databases, medium-sized database hosting, and memory-intensive data processing pipelines. It is also suitable for lightweight LLM text generation and web serving workloads that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage.
description: Vultr voc-m-16c-128gb-1600s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio. Built on the x86_64 AMD EPYC-Rome architecture running at 2.0 GHz, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model and includes 1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, though multi-core scaling and database operations remain in the average tier. The shared CPU model offers a cost-effective profile for memory-heavy workloads, though it introduces potential resource contention under sustained multi-core utilization. This server is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive data processing.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server combining high RAM capacity and fast NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads on a shared CPU architecture.

128.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per vCPU
1600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for fast data access
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong memory benchmark performance
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive pipelines
